name: User Management
overview: Group policy templates, account lifecycle, password hygiene, and easier user onboarding for Jellyfin.
description: User Management provides user groups and password hygiene to accounts that already exist on your Jellyfin server. Define group permission templates with live sync, set account expiry and auto-disable inactive users, and enforce password requirements. Use these groups to send user-managed, and protected invite links.

versions.changelog:
## Features
- Groups can now template the web home screen section order - My Media, Continue Watching, Continue Listening, Continue Reading, Recently Added, Next Up, Live TV and Active Recordings - applied with the same Override/Inherit model as other settings (web client only; mobile and TV apps keep their own layout)

## Features
- The group library order now lists every home screen view, adding Live TV, Collections and other views that are not standalone libraries so it matches the Jellyfin dashboard

## Fixes
- The automatic folder grouping option now lists only the folders Jellyfin can group into Movies and TV views, instead of every library

## Features
- Groups gain a Configuration section that manages Jellyfin user preferences with the same Override/Inherit model as permissions: subtitle mode, preferred subtitle and audio language, play default audio track, remember audio and subtitle track selections, play next episode automatically, Google Cast version, and display missing episodes
- Groups can manage home screen settings: hide watched content from Recently Added, media library order, My Media and home-section visibility, and automatic folder grouping into Movies/Music/TV views
- Home screen library lists only show libraries the group grants access to, so a group cannot order or toggle a library it hides from members
- Reorganized the Groups page into four sections - Membership, Settings, Configuration, and Other

## Features
- Adds the ability to duplicate an existing invite

## Fixes
- Disallowing password changes could get users locked out
- Password resets should always be available to admins on others' behalf

## Security
- Remove reset password file path from the admin logs just in case

## Developer
- README cleanup
- Fix CodeQL

## Features
- Invite welcome message shown to invitees on the signup page
- Invite resources & link buttons presented to new users after signup
- Creates a tab for viewing forgot password requests
- Options to disallow password changes
- Shorter invite links at /invite/{token}
- Output logs to the Activity Logs for better visibility

## Security
- Groups that disallow password changes cannot take invites
- Locked or expired invites cannot be re-enabled until their group and expiration allow it
- Externally authenticated users (LDAP, SSO) are never enrolled in password rules
- Invite PIN hashes never leave the server and PIN entry fields are masked
- Reset code extraction is off by default
- HTTPS advisories where sensitive information is used
- Single group membership is now enforced by the frontend to mirror backend

## Fixes
- Invite pages serve the server favicon
- Password changes rejected by group rules fail fast instead of hanging the dialog (400 Error)
- Empty passwords are disallowed by default and the empty password priority is documented
- Force invite PINs to be be 6 digits (numbers only)

## Developer
- Provider enrollment switches through policy updates instead of direct entity writes
- Invite token comparison is constant time
- Invite usage, PIN failure, and rate limit counters move to a standalone status file (Invites & Groups stored separately)

## Developer
- Bump to base 2026.6.23
- Default to appropriate casing of URLs
- File cleanup

## Fixes
- Expired invites waited for the scheduled task to disallow usage

## Developer
- Migrate shared components to base
- Bump to base 2026.6.15

## Fixes
- Allow locking/unlocking invites
- Allow editing an invite's expiration date
- Add a media conversion permission to groups
- Keep expired and inactive users disabled when a group would otherwise re-enable them
- Fix poor formatting

## Notice
- Due to the changes in https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/pull/15368 this plugin will only work for 10.11.9 or higher

## Fixes
- The manifest group was general instead of administration
- The description was just plain wrong from an earlier revision
- Cleanup visuals for record counts
- Fixes for user management changes in 10.11.9+

### Initial Release

This plugin was built with the goal of automating some of the painpoints that I had managing users. The primary focuses are groups for permissions and configurations to make adding libraries/changes easier, and invite links that are part of Jellyfin instead of using an external platform.
